# Hollow top-down placeholder sprite sheets

**Functional blockout stand-in — not final art.** Generated by
`tools/gen_hollow_placeholder_sheet.py` so the Hollow can be wired into an
`AnimatedSprite2D` later, replacing its `Polygon2D` blockout. Swap in real
painterly art later (see `CHARACTER_ASSET_PROMPTS_PASS_11.md`); the slicing below
stays the same as long as the grid matches.

To regenerate: `python tools/gen_hollow_placeholder_sheet.py`

## Grid

- **Cell:** 96 × 96 px, transparent background, no baked shadow.
- **Rows (top → bottom):** `down`, `up`, `left`, `right`  ← facing direction
  (canonical order, matches `CHARACTER_SPRITE_SPEC.md` and the player sheets).
- **Columns:** animation frames (left → right).
- Figure centred; consistent feet baseline; origin = cell centre (aligns to the
  Hollow body centre, 22 × 30 collision).

| File | Size | H frames | V frames | Frames/dir |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `hollow_idle.png`   | 384 × 384 | 4 | 4 | 4 |
| `hollow_walk.png`   | 576 × 384 | 6 | 4 | 6 |
| `hollow_attack.png` | 384 × 384 | 4 | 4 | 4 |
| `hollow_hit.png`    | 192 × 384 | 2 | 4 | 2 |
| `hollow_death.png`  | 576 × 384 | 6 | 4 | 6 |

`hollow_topdown_preview.png` is a stacked eyeball reference only — do **not** use
it in-game.

## Style / readability

- Pale, gaunt, **forgotten humanoid** silhouette: bone/grey-green body, a dark
  hollow void at the chest, faint cyan eyes, and cold cyan corruption wisps.
- **No red aura.** Reads against dark ash ground; clearly distinct from the
  warm-brown, backpacked player (taller, thinner, no coat/pack).
- `hollow_death.png` is a 6-frame **disperse** (intact → break-apart into ash +
  cyan sparks with fading alpha).

## Slicing into SpriteFrames (Godot 4)

For each file: *SpriteFrames → Add frames from sprite sheet*, **Horizontal = H
frames**, **Vertical = 4**. Each row is one direction. Name animations
`<anim>_<dir>` (`idle_down`, `walk_left`, `attack_up`, `hit_right`,
`death_down`, …). Loop `idle_*`/`walk_*`; leave `attack_*`/`hit_*`/`death_*`
non-looping.

Suggested node: keep the script-referenced decor nodes (`ForgottenHaze`,
`HitSpark`, `ContactShadow`) and add the `AnimatedSprite2D` as the visual — see
`CHARACTER_ART_INTEGRATION_CHECKLIST.md`. The engine already applies a red hurt
`modulate` and a cyan death fade, so the sheet frames stay neutral/pale.

## Note on the death sheet

The original `CHARACTER_SPRITE_SPEC.md` sketched death as a single
direction-agnostic 5-frame strip. This placeholder instead provides a
**per-direction 6-frame** `death` (4 × 6) so the disperse can face the way the
Hollow died. At integration you may use just one row if you prefer a
direction-agnostic death; the extra rows are harmless.
